typecheck.details: No mypy/pyright installed; py_compile syntax check on all 11 Python source files passed

lint.details: No ruff/flake8/pylint installed; py_compile syntax check on all Python files passed

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify the 'HTML escaping is mandatory' convention for markdown rendering contexts. Add guidance like: 'For markdown preview/rendering, escape HTML entities in source text before applying markdown transformations. Fenced code blocks should always use escapeHtml(). Inline content (headings, paragraphs, list items) should have non-markdown HTML tags escaped to prevent XSS.'
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature claude-md-editor's renderInline() function in static/app.js applies regex markdown transformations without first escaping HTML. Only fenced code blocks call escapeHtml(). This creates a theoretical XSS vector in the CLAUDE.md preview. Both reviewers flagged this pattern.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the DB connection pattern used in web.py: endpoints use bare conn = memory_db.get_conn() / conn.close() without try/finally. Consider recommending a context manager pattern for new code to prevent connection leaks on exceptions, while acknowledging the existing pattern.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Feature facts-crud-api-and-ui added 3 new endpoints following the existing bare open/close pattern from pre-existing endpoints. Reviewer noted connection leak potential if exceptions occur between open and close.

rejectedObservations.observation: Project filter uses LIKE (contains) while category uses exact match (=). This inconsistency is established in the existing search endpoint.
rejectedObservations.reason: This is a pre-existing design choice that was already established before this milestone. The new code correctly follows the existing convention. Documenting it would be low value — it's a minor implementation detail, not a pattern that causes confusion across features.
